title: gilia tenuiflora var. arenaria
intro: Gilia tenuiflora var. arenaria, also known as the white top or Arizona gilia, is a rare and unique plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial herb with small white flowers and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, including its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. Gilia tenuiflora var. arenaria is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers are used in traditional medicine to create various remedies.
